concerns.text: Phase 6 (Tasks T036–T039) introduces a 'Tractography Confidence Sensitivity' analysis and reviewer revision workflow that has no basis in spec.md (which only mandates window length and density threshold sensitivity in FR-006/FR-008). This constitutes unapproved scope creep and potential implementation waste, violating the constraint that tasks must not introduce features not demanded by the spec or plan.

Task T016 describes a generic 'sliding-window correlation' implementation but omits the critical parameters mandated by spec.md FR-002: '30 TR window, 1 TR step' and 'concatenating these windowed matrices across all subjects'. The task deliverable falls short of the FR it claims to satisfy, risking a non-compliant implementation.

Task T017 implements LOO K-Means but the description is ambiguous regarding the 'exclude i' logic compared to the precise mathematical definition in plan.md ('centroids are calculated by clustering the windowed matrices of all subjects j != i'). This semantic weakening risks violating Constitution Principle VI (Structural-Functional Independence) if implemented incorrectly.

Task T031 mentions 'window length (TR vs. multiple TRs)' which is vague. spec.md FR-006 and SC-002 specifically require a comparison between a 30 TR baseline and a 20 TR sensitivity check. The task must explicitly reference these values to ensure the specific robustness check mandated by the spec is performed.
